mechanism_narrative: The level of real retail sales (RSAFS) is a direct proxy for consumer demand strength. Higher consumer spending supports economic growth expectations, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield higher through the real rate channel over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is not positively correlated with RSAFS level (Spearman rank correlation < 0.1) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.0%, it signals a recessionary environment that increases demand for safe-haven government bonds, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, the 20-day forward yield change is not predominantly negative (less than 60% of occurrences yield a decline).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of high inflation (CPIAUCSL above 4%) and slowing industrial production (INDPRO negative monthly change) creates a stagflationary regime. In such conditions, markets expect monetary tightening that depresses growth expectations, leading to lower 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold simultaneously, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in fewer than 60% of instances over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of economic contraction (identified by two consecutive quarterly declines in GDPC1), a drop in industrial production (INDPRO) amplifies recession fears and heightens demand for duration, pushing 10-year yields lower. In expansion regimes, the same drop in INDPRO is temporary and does not drive yields down.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: During recession regimes, the correlation between INDPRO declines and 20-day forward yield changes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative yield changes is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) directly reflects inflation dynamics. Higher inflation erodes the real value of fixed coupon payments, leading investors to demand higher nominal yields to compensate, causing the 10-year yield to rise over a 5-day horizon as the market prices in inflation expectations.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward yield change is not positively correlated with the level of CPIAUCSL (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST month-over-month drop exceeding 10%) signals a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown. This triggers a flight-to-quality into Treasuries, reducing the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST monthly decline exceeds 10%, the 20-day forward yield increases in more than 50% of cases, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and retail sales growth (RSAFS) determines the balance between monetary stance and demand momentum. When rates are low (< 2%) and retail sales growth is strong (> 2% month-over-month), the economy is overheating, leading to rate hike expectations that push 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the 20-day forward yield change is positive in fewer than 60% of observations over the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of very low unemployment (UNRATE below 4%), rising payrolls (PAYEMS increases) adds to inflationary pressure and reinforces expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise. In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), rising payrolls signals labor market improvement without inflation risk, and yields may not rise.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4% and PAYEMS increases, the 20-day forward yield change is not positive in at least 65% of occurrences over any rolling 3-year period.
